entry.title: Memory/Cost estimation in Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

We are currently using SAP Solution Manager with multiple capabilities such as System Monitoring, Business Process Monitoring, ChaRM, and Solution Documentation. As part of our Cloud ALM migration planning, we are working on cost and memory/data estimation compared to our existing SolMan setup.

We understand that SAP Cloud ALM follows a fair-use model (~24 GB HANA memory per tenant) and does not store full historical data like Solution Manager. However, we are facing challenges in translating our current SolMan usage into Cloud ALM sizing and cost estimation.

Could you please help us with the following:

1. Memory / Data Estimation

  • What is the recommended approach or methodology to estimate Cloud ALM memory requirements based on current Solution Manager usage?
  • Which parameters should be considered (e.g., number of systems, BPMon objects, interfaces, alerts, retention)?
  • Are there any official SAP sizing tools, estimators, or benchmarks available to map SolMan usage to Cloud ALM memory consumption?

2. Cost for Cloud ALM (Beyond Fair Use)

  • If the 24 GB memory limit is exceeded, how is the cost calculated?
  • What is the pricing model for tenant extensions (additional memory / API limits), and are there any standard benchmarks for estimating these costs in advance?

3. Cost & Impact of Continuing Solution Manager (Post‑2027)

  • If we continue using SAP Solution Manager beyond 2027 (end of mainstream maintenance):
    • What additional extended maintenance charges or premiums will apply until 2030?
    • Are there any cost impacts for infrastructure, support, or customer-specific maintenance beyond standard SAP support?
  • From a cost and risk perspective, how does continuing SolMan until 2030 compare with migrating to Cloud ALM?

4. Planning Guidance

  • Are there any reference architectures, benchmarks, or typical memory/cost ranges for small, medium, and large landscapes that can help us estimate both Cloud ALM and SolMan continuation costs?

Any guidance or documentation would help us build a reliable cost and sizing estimation model for our migration planning.

entry.title: CRUD Operations with Image Upload using SAPUI5, Node.js, Express, MySQL, and Multer
entry.content:
Hi, 
 
Introduction: 

Media such as images, videos, and icons play an important role in making applications visually appealing and interactive, which helps improve user engagement. 

In this blog, we will learn how to perform CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) operations with image upload functionality using: 

  • SAPUI5 for the frontend  
  • Node.js and Express.js for the backend  
  • MySQL as the database  
  • Multer for handling file uploads 

Backend Implementation using Node.js

Prerequisite - Install MySQL Workbench/MySQL Server and create a database. 

 
Step 1 : Create a backend folder and inside it create a file named index.js 
And run the following commands  

  • npm init –y 
  • npm i express cors mysql2 multer 

 

Command 

Purpose 

npm init -y 

Initializes a Node.js project with default configurations 

npm i express 

Installs Express.js framework for backend APIs 

npm i cors 

Enables Cross-Origin Resource Sharing between frontend and backend 

npm i mysql2 

Connects Node.js application with MySQL database 

npm i multer 

Handles file uploads 

 
Step 2 : Create the uploads folder 

Note: Make sure the uploads folder exists before starting the server. 
Otherwise, multer may throw an error while uploading files. 

 
Step 3 : Add the below code in index.js 

Note : Enter the MySQL password (the one you created during MySQL Workbench/MySQL server Installation)

const express = require("express")
const cors = require("cors")
const mysql = require("mysql2")
const multer = require("multer")

const app = express()

app.use(express.json())
app.use(cors())

// Database Connection
const db = mysql.createConnection({
    host: 'localhost',
    user: 'root',
    password: 'Root@1234',
    database: 'student_db'
})

db.connect((err) => {
    if (err) {
        console.log("Database Connection Failed")
        console.log(err.message)
    } else {
        console.log("Database Connected Successfully")
    }
})

// Create Table
db.query(`CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users(
id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
name VARCHAR(50),
city VARCHAR(50),
image VARCHAR(255)
)`, (err) => {
    if (err) {
        console.log("Table Creation Failed")
    } else {
        console.log("Users Table Ready")
    }
})


// Multer Storage
const storage = multer.diskStorage({
    destination: (req, file, cb) => {
        cb(null, "uploads")
    },
    filename: (req, file, cb) => {
        cb(null, Date.now() + "-" + file.originalname)
    }
})

const upload = multer({
    storage: storage
})


// Insert User
app.post("/users", upload.single("image"), (req, res) => {

    let name = req.body.name
    let city = req.body.city
    let image = req.file.filename

    db.query(
        "INSERT INTO users(name,city,image) VALUES(?,?,?)",
        [name, city, image],
        (err) => {
            if (err) {
                console.log("Failed to Insert User")
                return res.send(err)
            }

            console.log(`User Inserted : ${name}`)
            res.send("Inserted")
        }
    )
})


// Get Users
app.get("/users", (req, res) => {

    db.query("SELECT * FROM users", (err, result) => {

        if (err) {
            console.log("Failed to Fetch Users")
            return res.send(err)
        }

        console.log("Users Data Fetched")
        res.send(result)
    })
})


// Update User
app.put("/users/:id", upload.single("image"), (req, res) => {

    let id = req.params.id
    let name = req.body.name
    let city = req.body.city

    if (req.file) {

        let image = req.file.filename

        db.query(
            "UPDATE users SET name=?,city=?,image=? WHERE id=?",
            [name, city, image, id],
            (err) => {

                if (err) {
                    console.log(`Failed to Update User ID : ${id}`)
                    return res.send(err)
                }

                console.log(`User Updated With Image : ID ${id}`)
                res.send("Updated")
            }
        )

    } else {

        db.query(
            "UPDATE users SET name=?,city=? WHERE id=?",
            [name, city, id],
            (err) => {

                if (err) {
                    console.log(`Failed to Update User ID : ${id}`)
                    return res.send(err)
                }

                console.log(`User Updated : ID ${id}`)
                res.send("Updated")
            }
        )
    }
})


// Delete User
app.delete("/users/:id", (req, res) => {

    let id = req.params.id

    db.query(
        "DELETE FROM users WHERE id=?",
        [id],
        (err) => {

            if (err) {
                console.log(`Failed to Delete User ID : ${id}`)
                return res.send(err)
            }

            console.log(`User Deleted : ID ${id}`)
            res.send("Deleted")
        }
    )
})


// Static Folder
app.use("/uploads", express.static("uploads"))


// Server Start
app.listen(3000, () => {
    console.log("Server Running Successfully")
    console.log("URL : http://localhost:3000")
})

Code explanation - Initially, all the required packages are imported. Then a connection to MySQL is established, and an uploads folder is used to store uploaded images. 

Here, the database name is student_db. After verifying the database connection, the application creates a table named users table inside the database (if table does not exists). 

Multer stores uploaded files on the server disk. All uploaded images are saved inside the uploads folder. To avoid duplicate file names, the uploaded image name is prefixed with the current timestamp using Date.now(). 

 
const upload = multer({ 
    storage: storage 
}) 

This connects multer with storage. 

upload.single("image") 

This middleware is used to upload a single file. 

  • "image" represents the field name  
  • The frontend or Postman form-data key must also be named image 

Then create API calls for Create, Read, Update and Delete 

app.use("/uploads", express.static("uploads")) 

This line exposes the uploads folder as a static directory so that uploaded images can be accessed directly through a URL. 

Example: 

uploads/17434img.png 

Access via: 

http://localhost:port_number/uploads/img_name.jpg 

The listen() method starts the server. 
The first parameter represents the port number on which the application runs. 

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 

Frontend Implementation using SAPUI5

Create the SAPUI5 application using basic template. 

In the view, create a form that accepts: 

  • Name  
  • City  
  • Image upload 

To upload images in SAPUI5, we use: 

  • Library: sap.ui.unified  
  • Control: FileUploader 

Three buttons are created for performing below operations: 

  • Create  
  • Update  
  • Delete  

 
After submitting the form, the user data is displayed in a list along with: 

  • User image  
  • Name  
  • City 

This is View1.view.xml file

<mvc:View controllerName="imgupdismulter.controller.View1"
    xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc"
    xmlns:u="sap.ui.unified"
    xmlns="sap.m">
    <Page id="page" title="{i18n>title}">
        <VBox class="sapUiSmallMargin">
            <HBox justifyContent="End" class="sapUiSmallMarginBottom">
                <Button text="Update" press="onUpdate" class="sapUiTinyMarginEnd"/>
                <Button text="Delete" press="onDelete" />
            </HBox>
            <!-- Form -->
            <Input id="name" placeholder="Enter Name" required="true" />
            <Input id="city" placeholder="Enter City" required="true" />
            <u:FileUploader 
                id="fileUploader" 
                name="image" 
                change="onFileSelect" required="true"/>
            <Button text="Create" press="onCreate" class="sapUiTinyMarginEnd"/>
            <List id="userList" />
        </VBox>
    </Page>
</mvc:View>


View Explanation 

  • Input controls are used for entering user details.  
  • FileUploader is used to select images.  
  • Button controls trigger CRUD operations.  
  • List displays all user records.  
  • Image control shows image preview before upload. 


This is View1.controller.js file

sap.ui.define([
    "sap/ui/core/mvc/Controller",
    "sap/m/MessageToast",
    "sap/m/StandardListItem"
], (Controller, MessageToast, StandardListItem) => {
    "use strict";

    return Controller.extend("imgupdismulter.controller.View1", {

        onInit() {
            this.loadData();
        },

        loadData: function () {
            debugger
            fetch("http://localhost:3000/users")
                .then(res => res.json())
                .then(data => {
                    let list = this.byId("userList");
                    list.removeAllItems();

                    data.forEach(user => {
                        list.addItem(
                            new StandardListItem({
                                title: user.name,
                                description: user.city,
                                icon: "http://localhost:3000/uploads/" + user.image,
                                type: "Active",
                                press: () => {
                                    debugger
                                    this.selectedId = user.id;
                                    this.byId("name").setValue(user.name);
                                    this.byId("city").setValue(user.city);
                                }
                            })
                        );
                    });
                });
        },

        onFileSelect: function (e) {
            debugger
            let file = e.getParameter("files")[0];
            this.selectedFile = file;
            this.selectedFileName = file.name;

            let reader = new FileReader();
            reader.onload = (evt) => {
                this.byId("preview").setSrc(evt.target.result);
            };
            reader.readAsDataURL(file);
        },

        onCreate: function () {

            let oName = this.byId("name");
            let oCity = this.byId("city");
            let oFile = this.byId("fileUploader");

            let name = oName.getValue().trim();
            let city = oCity.getValue().trim();

            // Regex
            let nameRegex = /^[A-Za-z ]{3,30}$/;
            let cityRegex = /^[A-Za-z ]{2,30}$/;

            // Reset Value States
            oName.setValueState("None");
            oCity.setValueState("None");
            oFile.setValueState("None");

            let isValid = true;

            // Name Validation
            if (!name) {

                oName.setValueState("Error");
                oName.setValueStateText("Name is required");

                isValid = false;

            } else if (!nameRegex.test(name)) {

                oName.setValueState("Error");
                oName.setValueStateText(
                    "Name must contain only letters and spaces (3-30 characters)"
                );

                isValid = false;
            }

            // City Validation
            if (!city) {

                oCity.setValueState("Error");
                oCity.setValueStateText("City is required");

                isValid = false;

            } else if (!cityRegex.test(city)) {

                oCity.setValueState("Error");
                oCity.setValueStateText(
                    "City must contain only letters and spaces (2-30 characters)"
                );

                isValid = false;
            }

            // File Validation
            if (!this.selectedFile) {

                oFile.setValueState("Error");
                oFile.setValueStateText("Image is required");

                isValid = false;
            }

            // Stop execution if validation fails
            if (!isValid) {

                MessageToast.show("Please enter valid data");

                return;
            }

            let formData = new FormData();

            formData.append("name", name);
            formData.append("city", city);
            formData.append("image", this.selectedFile);

            fetch("http://localhost:3000/users", {
                method: "POST",
                body: formData
            }).then(() => {

                MessageToast.show("Created Successfully");

                this.loadData();

                // Clear fields
                oName.setValue("");
                oCity.setValue("");
                oFile.clear();

                this.byId("preview").setSrc("");

                this.selectedFile = null;
                this.selectedFileName = null;
            });
        },

        onUpdate: function () {
            let name = this.byId("name").getValue();
            let city = this.byId("city").getValue();

            let formData = new FormData();
            formData.append("name", name);
            formData.append("city", city);

            if (this.selectedFile)
                formData.append("image", this.selectedFile);

            fetch("http://localhost:3000/users/" + this.selectedId, {
                method: "PUT",
                body: formData
            }).then(() => {
                MessageToast.show("Updated");
                this.loadData();
            });
        },

        onDelete: function () {
            fetch("http://localhost:3000/users/" + this.selectedId, {
                method: "DELETE"
            }).then(() => {
                MessageToast.show("Deleted");
                this.loadData();
            });
        }
    });
});

Controller Logic Explanation :

  • onInit() loads existing user data when the application starts.  
  • loadData() fetches all records from the backend and displays them in the SAPUI5 list.  
  • onFileSelect() reads the selected image and displays a preview.  
  • onCreate() sends form data to the backend using a POST request.  
  • onUpdate() updates existing user details using a PUT request.  
  • onDelete() removes a user record using a DELETE request. 

Note - First run the backend project using cli in the terminal ---- npm start or node index.js
make sure that "Database Connected Successfully" and running in the mentioned port ,
then run frontend project using cli in the terminal ---- npm run start 

Output :
After creation 

After updating

After Deleting

Conclusion : In this blog, we implemented CRUD operations with image handling using SAPUI5, Node.js, Express.js, MySQL, and Multer. 

The application allows users to: 

  • Upload images  
  • Store user information in MySQL  
  • Retrieve and display images dynamically  
  • Update existing records  
  • Delete records efficiently  

Multer is used for handling file uploads, while Express APIs manage backend operations. SAPUI5 provides a simple and interactive frontend for managing user data and displaying uploaded images.

entry.title: Re: Restrict Maintenance Notification conversion to only...
entry.content:
Good day @tathyaprakash_sahood9d589 

Thank you for your question.

Your concern is valid. In Public Cloud, restriction fields such as AUFART are not designed to only control one button or one process step inside one app, such as notification to order conversion in Manage Maintenance Notifications and the reason is because they are authorized restrictions.

Note that if you use the same restriction type for Order Type in more than one business catalog, then restriction it in a business role can also affect other apps that use the same authorisation object or restriction type.

So, to put that in simple terms:

  • Restricting AUFART in the catalog or role is not a clean way to control only notification conversion.
  • It may also restrict direct maintenance order creation if the same user role contains the maintenance order management catalog.
  • If the same user needs unrestricted direct order creation but restricted notification conversion, you cannot achieve this clearly with only catalog level AUFART restrictions.
  • You should follow a role design approach, where your user group that converts notifications only has access to the Reactive Maintenance order Type.
  • If the same user must create other order types directly but must not use them during notification conversion, then the current process has a gap here. You may consider submitting an enhancement request for that.

Here is what i recommend:

  1. Do not restrict AUFART broadly in a shared planner role. Rather maintain the AUFART restriction in a shared role that contains both SAP_EAM_BC_MN_MNG_PC (used for managing maintenance notifications) and SAP_EAM_BC_MP_ORD_MNG_PC (used to manage order maintenance or creation). This can create the cross-app impact you described, because the same order type restriction can influence value help, create authorisation and write authorisation in other maintenance order apps.                                   
  2. Splitting the responsibilities is the cleanest standard design. (note: this works if the user groups are different) For example:
    1. Role A: is a notification conversion role. It contains the notification management catalog, restricts order type to the Reactive Maintenance order type only and used by planners who should convert notifications only into Reactive Maintenance orders.
    2. Role B: creates direct order. It contains the maintenance order management catalog, allows additional maintenance order types and assigned only to users who are allowed to create those order types directly.                                        
  3. Use the Reactive Maintenance process design where possible. This means your target process is aligned with the standard Reactive Maintenance flow. If your business wants all notification-based orders to be Reactive Maintenance orders, then follow this best practice:
    1. Use the Reactive Maintenance process for work driven by notification.
    2. Restrict the users who convert notifications to the Reactive Maintenance order type.
    3. Allow direct order creation of other order types only through separate roles or separate user groups.
    4. Don’t give the same use both restricted conversion role and an unrestricted maintenance order creation role unless you confirm it through tests.
  4. If the same user must have both behaviours, then consider raising this as an enhancement. See KBA 2963059 on how to go about that.

 

In summary:

  • I suggest you use a role-based process separation.
  • If both roles are required for the same person, then standard authorisation restriction is not precise enough for your requirement.
  • In that case, I would not recommend forcing it through AUFART restriction because of the cross app impact.

Here are references for more guidance

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2dfa044a255f49e89a3050daf3c61c11/c4edd426410a4aa29ee9aa6448b4aeb5.html?locale=en-US

https://learning.sap.com/courses/implementing-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-asset-management/explaining-reactive-maintenance-4hh-

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: How can you delete user in Service and Asset Manager
entry.content:
Hi,

You can delete Users in the backend with the T-Code /N/SYCLO/CORE_PURGE.

There you can select your mobile application. You need the user GUID from the admin panel. Then you can check the "User Registry" checkbox. If you give a time in the future, the user will be deleted completely. Make sure to uncheck the "Test Run" at the bottom, if everything is set up correctly.

I hope this helps.

Regards

Philip

entry.title: Handling Concurrent Updates in CAP Java
entry.content:
The Problem: Lost Updates

Consider two users, Alice and Bob, accessing the same employee record simultaneously.

Alice updates the employee’s email address and saves the record.
Shortly afterward, Bob updates the employee’s last name and saves his changes.

However, Bob’s update was based on an earlier version of the record, prior to Alice’s modification. As a result, his save operation unintentionally overwrites Alice’s update without any conflict detection or warning.

This scenario is known as a lost update - a common concurrency issue in multi-user systems where one user’s changes silently overwrite another’s, leading to data integrity problems.


Why Not Just Lock the Record?

One way to prevent lost updates is pessimistic locking. As soon as Alice opens the record, the system locks it, preventing others from modifying it until she finishes.

While effective in theory, this approach is poorly suited for web applications. Users may leave forms open for extended periods, causing records to remain locked unnecessarily and blocking other users from making legitimate updates.

Under real-world traffic, long-held locks can quickly become a source of contention, reduced concurrency, and poor user experience.

The Solution: Optimistic Locking

Optimistic locking takes a different approach to concurrency control. Instead of locking records when they are read, the system allows multiple users to access and modify data concurrently.

The validation happens at save time. Before applying an update, the system checks whether the record has changed since the user originally retrieved it.

  • If the record has been modified in the meantime, the update is rejected to prevent overwriting newer data.
  • If the record is unchanged, the update succeeds and the record’s version is advanced.

This approach avoids long-held locks while still protecting the application from lost updates and unintended data overwrites.

How It Works: ETags

Optimistic locking on the web is implemented using ETags, a standard HTTP mechanism.

When a record is retrieved, the server includes an ETag in the response. This acts as a version identifier that changes whenever the record is updated.

When sending an update, the client includes this ETag in the If-Match header, effectively stating: apply this update only if the record is still on the same version I read.

The server then compares the provided ETag with the current version:

  • Match → The record is unchanged, the update is applied, and a new ETag is generated
  • Mismatch → The record has been modified by someone else, and the request is rejected with 412 Precondition Failed

This ensures updates are applied only to the latest version of the data, preventing silent overwrites.

How CAP Java Does It

CAP Java handles all of this automatically. We just need to tell the framework which field to use as the ETag.

We can do that with a single annotation in our CDS model:


The managed aspect automatically maintains the modifiedAt timestamp, updating it whenever the record is changed. By annotating this field with @odata.etag, CAP uses it as the entity’s version identifier (ETag).

From that point onward, concurrency control is handled automatically. For every update request, CAP validates the incoming If-Match header against the current value of modifiedAt. If the values differ, the framework rejects the update with an HTTP 412 Precondition Failed response, preventing stale data from overwriting newer changes.

No additional Java code is required.

Seeing It in Action

Let us start the application and create an employee record:


As we can see the response contains the ETag value: 2026-05-25T06:55:44.400909Z

Let us simulate Alice’s request of updating the email with the ETag we just received:


This succeeds. The record is updated and a new ETag is generated.

Now, simulating Bob's request (who is working with the older version) using the older ETag value.


Bob's request is rejected with 412 Precondition Failed.
Alice's change is safe.

What Happens Next: Resolving the Conflict

A 412 Precondition Failed response is not an error to suppress - it is a signal that the data has changed since it was last read.
At this point, the user can reload the latest version of the data, review the differences, and reapply their changes with full context before saving again.

Hence, Bob retrieves the latest version of the record and obtains the updated ETag value.


Bob resubmits his update using the fresh ETag:  2026-05-25T07:07:15.691711Z



This time it succeeds. Both changes are now in the record. Nothing was lost.

The key insight is that a 412 is not a failure - it is a safety net doing its job. The system caught a potential lost update before it happened and gave Bob the opportunity to resolve it cleanly.

Conclusion

Concurrency-related data integrity issues are particularly problematic because they occur silently. When two users update the same record, the later save succeeds and the earlier change is overwritten without any indication of a conflict.

Optimistic locking is the standard approach to preventing this class of issue, and CAP Java provides built-in support. By adding a simple @odata.etag annotation, the framework can automatically detect stale updates and reject conflicting changes before any data is lost.

It is a minimal change to the data model, but one that significantly improves the consistency and reliability of enterprise applications.

entry.title: E-Invoicing for self billing in France E-Invoice
entry.content: We are implementing SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) for France E‑Invoicing. As part of the mandate, we are required to use self‑invoicing; however, SAP currently does not provide a standard solution for this scenario. According to French compliance requirements, self‑invoices must be generated by us through MRRL and subsequently transmitted to the approved Plateformes de Dématérialisation Partenaires (PAs). We kindly request your guidance and a proposed solution to address this gap, as this is critical for ensuring compliance with the French e‑invoicing law. Thank you in advance for your support and prompt attention to this matter.

entry.title: SAP Sapphire 2026: The Autonomous Enterprise Has Arrived
entry.content:
SAP Sapphire 2026: The Autonomous Enterprise Has Arrived

A complete summary of the biggest AI announcements from Orlando, May 13–14, 2026

50+

Joule Assistants

200+

Specialized Agents

€100M

Partner AI Fund

26

Industries

SAP Sapphire 2026 in Orlando marked what many are calling the most significant shift in enterprise software in decades. CEO Christian Klein unveiled the Autonomous Enterprise — a bold repositioning of SAP from 'software that records what people do' to 'software that does the work itself.' With over 50 Joule Assistants and 200+ specialized AI agents shipping across Finance, Spend Management, Supply Chain, HR, and Customer Experience, SAP is making a clear statement: the era of autonomous business operations is here.

This blog post covers all 12 major announcement areas from the event, based on official SAP News Center announcements.

1. The Autonomous Enterprise — Christian Klein's Vision

"Eighty percent accuracy isn't sufficient when running mission-critical businesses. AI must work at enterprise grade — accurate, compliant, and secure."  — Christian Klein, CEO, SAP

The keynote set the tone for the entire conference. SAP's strategic pivot centers on three pillars:

  • SAP Business AI Platform — A unified build-and-govern layer combining BTP, Business Data Cloud, and AI Foundation with SAP Knowledge Graph at its core
  • SAP Autonomous Suite — 200+ specialized agents and 50+ Joule Assistants across all major business functions, shipping now
  • Joule Work — A completely new human interface replacing traditional app navigation, described as the most significant UX shift since Fiori

A live on-stage demo with H&M showcased Store Intelligence Agents and an AI-powered InStore Concierge delivering personalized product recommendations — a compelling real-world proof point of the vision.

2. SAP Business AI Platform — One Foundation

BTP, Business Data Cloud, and AI Foundation have been unified into a single architecture, with the SAP Knowledge Graph at the core:

01

SAP Knowledge Graph: 452,000 SAP tables encoded; 7.3 million data fields with semantic relationships; 50 years of SAP ERP engineering

02

Anthropic Claude: Named primary AI reasoning model powering Joule — multi-step reasoning for enterprise-grade workflows with full security and compliance controls

03

Joule Studio 2.0 (GA Now): AI-first dev environment integrating n8n visual workflow, Vercel design tooling, and NVIDIA OpenShell secure runtime

04

SAP AI Agent Hub (Q3 2026): Central marketplace for discovering, managing, and governing AI agents — 680+ partner submissions already received

05

Prior Labs / SAP-RPT-1.5: Tops TabArena benchmark — 3.5x better than LLMs on structured enterprise predictions

06

Dremio (Q3 2026): Open Lakehouse acquisition extending Business Data Cloud with open-format analytics at scale

07

Microsoft Fabric Connect (GA): SAP data flows natively into Microsoft Fabric without duplication — zero-copy integration

08

AWS Zero-Copy (GA): SAP data accessible in Amazon Athena — no data movement required

3. Joule Work — The New Human Interface

Joule Work abandons traditional app navigation entirely. Describe outcomes in natural language and Joule coordinates data, workflows, and agents to execute. This is the biggest UX shift since Fiori.

Availability

  • Mobile App — Available Now: Full Joule Work experience on mobile devices
  • Web Client — Early Adopter Care: GA targeted for H2 2026
  • Desktop App — Planned Q2 2026: Bridges SAP with local files, email, and documents
  • Voice via LiveKit — Early Adopter Care: Real-time voice AI for field, factory, and warehouse

Interoperability Protocols

  • Model Context Protocol (MCP) — Available Now: Joule connects natively to Microsoft Copilot and third-party agents
  • Agent-to-Agent (A2A) Protocol — GA Q4 2026: Bidirectional integration with Salesforce Agentforce and ServiceNow

4. Autonomous Finance — 7 Joule Assistants (GA Q3 2026)

"72% of organizations find financial planning too time-consuming."  — SAP Research

Finance operations follow a Sense–Reason–Act model: detect signals, evaluate constraints in real time, connect plans to execution — automatically. The 7 Joule Assistants cover the full financial lifecycle:

01

Financial Closing Assistant: Compresses close cycle from weeks to days

02

Financial Planning Assistant: Continuous planning with SAP Analytics Cloud

03

Billing Assistant: Detects inconsistencies before invoice generation

04

Governance Assistant: CSRD and regulatory compliance tracking

05

Tax & Compliance Assistant: BEPS Pillar 2 and global e-invoicing mandates

06

Accounts Receivable Assistant: Churn prediction and automated dunning

07

Cash & Treasury Assistant: Real-time liquidity visibility and allocation

SAP Enterprise Planning is the new flagship product — moving finance from periodic planning cycles to continuous steering, with Joule Agents embedded to monitor signals and recommend actions automatically.

5. Autonomous Spend Management — 11 Joule Assistants

The full source-to-pay lifecycle is now covered by 11 Joule Assistants, addressing long-standing fragmentation between procurement, finance, and operations. Key milestones include Ariba Buying reaching GA and Fieldglass introducing AI-enhanced contractor management. Assistants span Category Management, Sourcing, Contract Management, Supplier Collaboration, Purchase Requisitions, Invoice Processing, Expense Management, Travel Management, Workforce Planning, Contingent Workforce, and Spend Analytics.

6. Autonomous Supply Chain — 6 Joule Assistants + 60+ Agents

Supply chain sees 6 Joule Assistants alongside 60+ specialized agents covering demand planning, production scheduling, logistics, and asset management. Notable highlights:

  • NVIDIA Partnership — Factory-edge AI for sub-second quality inspection and anomaly detection
  • RWE Demo — Offshore wind turbine maintenance using autonomous asset management agents
  • Assistants cover: Demand Planning, Production Scheduling, Logistics, Inventory Management, Supplier Risk, and Asset Maintenance

7. Autonomous HCM — 13 Joule Assistants (All GA June 2026)

Human Capital Management gets the largest single-function rollout — 13 Joule Assistants all reaching General Availability in June 2026. The suite covers the complete employee lifecycle from recruiting through retirement, with notable integrations:

  • SmartRecruiters Integration — AI-enhanced talent acquisition workflows
  • Workforce Planning — Predictive headcount and skills gap analysis
  • Full coverage: Recruiting, Onboarding, Learning, Performance, Succession, Compensation, Payroll, Benefits, Time & Attendance, Employee Experience, HR Service Delivery, Analytics, and Workforce Planning

8. Autonomous Customer Experience — 11 Joule Assistants

The CX suite is built on the principle that every customer interaction reflects how well the business runs — AI must operate across the full execution chain. 11 Joule Assistants cover marketing through service:

  • Content, Campaign, Merchandising, Shopping, Order Management
  • Sales, Deal Qualification, Deal Closing
  • Case Management, Service Management, Content Generation

Key CX Updates

  • SAP Commerce Cloud for Mid-Market — GA Q3 2026
  • Composable Commerce Services — New modular cart and checkout
  • Google Cloud — Gemini Enterprise + Universal Commerce Protocol for multimodal personalized journeys
  • Parloa — Voice and digital self-service in SAP Service Cloud
  • Adyen / Checkout.com / PayPal — Embedded payment processing in SAP Commerce workflows

9. Migration & Modernisation — 35%+ Effort Reduction

 

35%+

ERP Migration Effort Reduction

3

Joule Assistants in RISE Year 1

3

S/4HANA Release Cycles Certified

 

  • RISE with SAP Restructured — Now activates 3 Joule Assistants contractually in Year 1; ECC customers can access select AI scenarios immediately before full migration completes
  • SAP GROW — Full Joule portfolio access from day one for mid-market cloud-first customers
  • Clean Core Certification — Formally certifies BTP extensions as upgrade-compatible across three S/4HANA Cloud release cycles
  • Palantir AIP Partnership (GA Q3 2026) — SAP Endorsed App status with Accenture as global strategic services partner

 

10. Strategic Partnerships Reshaping the Ecosystem

SAP announced 11 strategic alliances spanning AI reasoning, infrastructure, data, experience, and interoperability:

 

01

Anthropic: Claude named primary reasoning model powering Joule across the full Autonomous Suite

02

NVIDIA: OpenShell runtime for Joule Studio; factory-edge inference for sub-second quality inspection

03

Microsoft: Fabric Connect GA; A2A interoperability with Microsoft Copilot (Q4 2026)

04

Google Cloud: Gemini Enterprise for CX; Universal Commerce Protocol; Merchant Center integration

05

AWS: Zero-copy SAP data access via Amazon Athena — no data movement required

06

Palantir: AIP as SAP Endorsed App + Solution Extension; GA Q3 2026 with Accenture as global partner

07

Accenture: Global strategic services partner for Palantir AIP and complex S/4 migrations

08

n8n: Visual workflow builder integrated into Joule Studio for no-code agent design

09

Vercel: Design tooling in Joule Studio; accelerates storefront development for CX

10

LiveKit: Real-time voice AI for Joule Work in field, factory, and warehouse settings

11

Parloa: AI agents in SAP Service Cloud for end-to-end voice and digital service resolution

 

11. Partner Ecosystem — €100M Business AI Fund

"A Symphony of Partnership to Ring in the Era of the Autonomous Enterprise."  — Karl Fahrbach, Chief Partner Officer, SAP

The €100M Business AI Partner-Led Adoption Program is structured around four service packages:

  • Adoption — Entry level: deploy existing AI assistants with structured enablement
  • Launch — Build and deploy at least one custom agent via Joule Studio
  • Performance — Demonstrate measurable business outcomes from deployed agents
  • Enterprise — Comprehensive AI strategy delivery with full Joule Studio mastery

 

Additional milestones include a new Validated Partner Designation for AI and data strategy expertise, the Partner Agent Race to SAP TechEd with 680+ submissions, and a full enablement curriculum across sales, presales, consulting, and development roles.

 

12. Industry AI — 26 Sectors at GA

SAP is embedding domain logic directly into the Autonomous Suite for 26 industries. Seven sectors are live at launch:

  • Utilities — Autonomous asset management; RWE offshore wind turbine maintenance demo
  • Manufacturing — Factory-edge AI with NVIDIA; quality inspection and production excellence agents
  • Life Sciences — Compliance-aware manufacturing and regulatory documentation for pharma
  • Consumer & Retail — Unified commerce, revenue growth management, AI-powered in-store concierge (H&M live keynote)
  • Oil, Gas & Chemicals — Asset-intensive scenarios via Fieldglass; contractor lifecycle and credential verification
  • Engineering & Construction — Project and resource management with supply chain integration
  • + 20 More Industries — Agriculture, Banking, Insurance, and 17 others at full GA

 

Key Takeaways for the SAP Community

SAP Sapphire 2026 wasn't a roadmap event — it was a delivery event. The capabilities announced are shipping now or within the next two quarters. Here's what the SAP community needs to know:

 

  1. Joule Work changes how users interact with SAP

The shift from menu-driven navigation to natural language is fundamental. Partners and customers should begin planning adoption strategies now.

  1. Clean Core is now certifiable and incentivized

With Clean Core Certification and the RISE restructuring, there's never been a clearer path for ECC customers to modernize without losing access to AI capabilities during migration.

  1. The partner opportunity is enormous

The €100M fund, 680+ agent submissions, and four-tier adoption program create a massive opportunity for SAP partners to build, deploy, and monetize AI capabilities.

  1. Industry AI is real and customer-validated

H&M and RWE weren't just demos — they were production-grade live deployments on stage. This signals that enterprise-grade autonomous operations are already in production for SAP's largest customers.

Based on official SAP News Center announcements from SAP Sapphire 2026, Orlando, May 13–14, 2026.

For more information, visit community.sap.com

entry.title: Partner learning accelerator: SAP certified - Security administrator
entry.content:
Security and user access management are often behind the scenes.  Until something breaks.  Then everyone notices.

That’s why strong SAP security expertise matters more than ever across SAP S/4HANA and cloud environments.

Our upcoming “Partner Learning Accelerator: SAP Certified – Security Administrator” is designed to help SAP Partners prepare for the C_SEC certification through a focused 6-hour live session.  We’ll cover the topics partners deal with every day: authorization concepts, identity and access management, business role design, SAP Fiori authorizations, plus troubleshooting access issues across SAP systems.

The thing is, certification prep can feel overwhelming when you’re balancing customer projects and deadlines.  This session helps cut through the noise and focus on the exam domains that matter most.

If you already work with SAP system administration or user management, this is a great next step to sharpen your expertise and validate your skills.

Register now and join us.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2025/jun/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00010506.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #SAPSecurity #SAPS4HANA #IdentityAccessManagement #SAPLearning

entry.title: Autonomous Enterprise: SAP змінює правила гри — і допомагає вам підготуватися
entry.content:
На конференції SAP Sapphire 2026 компанія SAP офіційно оголосила про старт концепції Autonomous Enterprise (Автономне підприємство)— фундаментальну зміну того, як проектуються, управляються та функціонують компанії в епоху ШІ.

З чого складається Autonomous Enterprise?

  • SAP Business AI Platform об’єднує BTP, SAP Business Data Cloud та SAP Business AI в єдине середовище. В основі — SAP Knowledge Graph та Joule Studio для створення агентів у форматі no-code, pro-code або AI-фреймворків.
  • SAP Autonomous Suite включає 50+ асистентів Joule та 200+ агентів для автоматизації наскрізних процесів. Наприклад, Autonomous Close Assistant може скоротити фінансове закриття з кількох тижнів до кількох днів.
  • Joule Work — єдина точка взаємодії замість десятків застосунків. Користувач описує бажаний результат, а Joule координує процеси, дані та агентів. Доступно на десктопі, мобільних пристроях та через голосовий інтерфейс.

Інвестиція у 100 млн євро

SAP запускає фонд для прискорення переходу до Autonomous Enterprise:

  • Клієнти RISE with SAP — три активовані асистенти Joule в перший рік
  • Клієнти SAP GROW — повний доступ до портфоліо з першого дня
  • Нові інструменти міграції скорочують зусилля на перехід до хмарного ERP більш ніж на 35%

Безкоштовна серія вебінарів

На тлі оголошень із Sapphire SAP запускає безкоштовну серію вебінарів «Architecting the Autonomous Enterprise» — 11 сесій, розроблених архітекторами для архітекторів.

📅 1 червня — 12 серпня 2026, щотижня
🕔 18:00–19:00 за українським часом

Кожна сесія ведеться практиками, лідерами продуктів, клієнтами та запрошеними спікерами, які самі будують, управляють або консультують щодо тих самих архітектурних рішень. Формат — одна сфокусована тема на сесію з повноцінним Q&A.

Програма сесій:

Тема

Дата

Unpacking SAP’s Autonomous Enterprise for Architects

1 червня 2026

What SAP’s Evolved Portfolio Means for Architects

10 червня 2026

Architecting From Intent to Action with Joule Work & Knowledge Graph

16 червня 2026

Playbook for Building Your Own SAP Agents (Hands-On)

26 червня 2026

SAP’s Innovation Factory for Architecting and Activating AI

1 липня 2026

AI Agent Lifecycle Management at Enterprise Scale

8 липня 2026

An Architect’s Guide to the SAP Family of Models

14 липня 2026

Data Foundations for an Autonomous Enterprise

22 липня 2026

From Autonomous Domains to Industry AI: One Platform, Two Lenses

29 липня 2026

AI Governance and Security for an Autonomous Enterprise

5 серпня 2026

Role of an Enterprise Architect in Driving the Autonomous Enterprise

12 серпня 2026

* To be Finalised 
детальна інформація про спікерів та розклад - за посиланням

Для кого ця серія?
Для Enterprise, Solution, Data та AI Architects, Technology Leaders, а також SAP клієнтів і партнерів, що впроваджують або масштабують ШІ в корпоративних середовищах.

Що ви отримаєте?

  • Цілісну архітектурну модель Joule Work, SAP Autonomous Suite, Business AI Platform та Knowledge Graph
  •  Практичне керівництво з побудови та управління SAP-агентами
  •  Реальні кейси від Bayer та Henkel
  • Розуміння того, як роль Enterprise Architect еволюціонує в епоху ШІ

👉 Зареєструватися безкоштовно

Чому це важливо для українського бізнесу?

Autonomous Enterprise відкриває новий рівень конкурентоспроможності: скорочення операційних витрат, прискорення прийняття рішень та відповідність євроінтеграційним стандартам. Українські компанії мають унікальну можливість підготуватися до наступного етапу цифрової трансформації — вже зараз і безкоштовно.

Ера автономного підприємства вже настала. SAP дає всі інструменти, щоб бути серед перших.

memoryutil reflects RAM consumption relative to the assigned memory quota
cpuutil reflects CPU entitlement utilization

which metric is generally recommended for scaling decisions in such scenarios
whether scaling should primarily be driven by memory pressure or CPU utilization

Thanks
Kanika

entry.title: Re: Cash Flow Report Direct Method in S4 Public Cloud
entry.content:
Good day @sanne_larsen 

Thank you for your question.

The Generic Cash Flow Statement requires a valid cashflow statement version ID. The error message means that the value maintained in Setting Up Your Statutory Reporting ID 101071 is either missing or does not match a valid cashflow statement version. See KBA 3449130 for resolution details.

However, if Define Cash Flow Statement Version for Several Countries ID 105331 is not available in your system, then you cannot maintain the required cash flow statement version through that activity. Since the configuration activity is marked as Specialised for Certain Countries/Regions, it is only delivered for specific localised country or region scenarios. That means the limitation is not that J78 is inactive but that the required configuration activity is not available for the countries in your current scope.

For SE, DK, BE, NL and ES, where the cashflow statement is not a legal statutory requirement in your scenario, the standard DRC statutory report route may therefore not be the correct basis for an internal management cash flow report.

What I suggest you do instead is as follows:

  • Option 1, because you have the exact error mentioned in the KBA earlier and the required SSCUI is not available, i suggest you raise an incident and ask whether Generic Cash Flow Statement with SCCUI 105331 is supported for your scoped countries and release.
  • Option 2, if the requirement is internal management reporting and not legal reporting, consider using an analytical report approach instead of Run Statutory Reports.                                                                                                               

Depending on your reporting needs, the alternatives you have are:

  • Use standard cash management and liquidity reporting apps if your requirement is operational cash flow visibility.
  • Use financial statement/journal entry analytical reporting if the requirement is a structured finance report.
  • Build a custom analytical report based on journal entry line items and cash relevant account logic.
  • Use SAP Analytics Cloud or external reporting if you need a management format across multiple countries.

Note that DRC statutory reporting framework is normally best suited for legally defined country reports. If the countries do not require a statutory cash flow statement and the configuration activity for them is not delivered, using DRC as the base may not be the right approach.

 

See the following reference for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/4de200b26d5d42bbacae69c6bf6b9923/331dd0bd74fe480087e1deeec032aea0.html

 

  • Option 3 is for you to consider submitting an improvement request. See KBA 2963059 for more details.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Exploring the App “Origin of Goods” in SAP GTS
entry.content:
As an importer or a domestic operator, accurately collecting and documenting the origin of the externally-procured product and batches is of the utmost importance since it determines the duties, possible trade preference options available, and import restrictions that apply to the shipment.

As a matter of fact, the Country/Region of Origin is a mandatory attribute for Customs treatment, duty assessment, and anti-dumping.

Non-preferential origin refers to the country where a product is either wholly obtained or entirely produced. It can also be the last country where the product underwent substantial transformation. Minor or minimal processing carried out in another location does not usually change the product’s origin status.

With the Manage Country/Region of Origin app, you can manage the country and region of origin for your externally procured products directly in SAP GTS.

Let us explore how this new feature works in SAP GTS Edition for Hana.

How does the flow of information look like?

  • Since there is no batch information in the Import declaration, the link is retrieved from inbound delivery item.
  • Both Import declaration before and after GR are supported.
  • Inbound Delivery can be transferred to GTS even if Customs Management is not activated consuming a certain API.

How does the back-end configuration look like?

Activate the plant for Origin of Goods.

Origin can be determined based on three sources, namely LTSD, Import declaration and Inbound Delivery. Based on the order of preference, the sequence can be configured.

The three sources of information.

1. Long Term Supplier Declaration

2. Customs Import Declaration

3. Inbound Delivery

How does the determination work?

Once the source documents have been transferred from S/4 to GTS, the determination of Country/Region of Origin report should be executed or scheduled as a background job to create new entries or updates in Manage Country/Region of Origin App.

Similarly, update of Batch Stock can be executed or scheduled as a background job to capture batches with stock in the determination base.

Working with “Manage Country/Region of Origin” app.

Upon executing, determination result list is populated for products and/or batches.

Exploring further down for a particular product, in determination base, the relevant list of material documents is also listed. This is the result of the report “determination of country/region of origin”

The logic is influenced by the “Determination Method” selection in the customizing.

In case of batch managed products, report “Schedule update of Batch Stock” constantly updates the current quantity. When the stock becomes zero, the batch is not relevant anymore for determining the Country or Region and will be moved to “Completed Batches”

When multiple batches are used without any unique value and alert is raised and the Country of origin is left blank at the header.

If the last batch listed in the determination base is relevant and has stock, then it is also possible to change in edit mode and mark it as expended. A user justification will be needed in such cases. Where upon the alert will be removed and Country of Origin at header level will become RO.

Important aspects to consider.

  1. In case a GR falls within the validity of a released Inbound LTSD, but CoO hasn't been maintained in the LTSD, the LTSD shouldn't be displayed in the app. Same applies for Inbound Delivery.
  2. If Inbound LTSD is created and released and has CoO maintained, but the Product is in Edit Mode, the new value will not be populated immediately. A background job can be scheduled to rerun.
  3. A reissued LTSD containing CoO, overwrites the old LTSD as a source.
  4. For the LTSDs where an alternative LTSD issuer exist than the actual supplier from the GR, there will be no updates with the LTSD document in the app.
  5. If an processed GR in the Origin of Goods App is cancelled in the Feeder System, that GR will be moved to Completed Documents after execution of “Schedule Determination of Country/Region”.
  6. In case of batches, if a GR is marked as expended, the GR is moved to completed documents, but also the corresponding batch is moved to completed batches.
  7. Source Overview will be populated as long the source document contains origin information. If no source exist for a particular GR, or the source documents do not contain origin, or the source is a manual maintenance of an user, the Source Overview will be empty.

Finally, once a unique country/region of origin is populated at the header level of the app, it is then automatically transferred back to the product master data in S4.

Prerequisites

  • Private Cloud S/4HANA 2021 and higher.
  • GTS Edition for Hana 2025, SP1

Application Help:

Origin of Goods | SAP Help Portal

Configuration Guide

Business Scenarios in Origin of Goods | SAP Help Portal

Administrator Guide

Origin of Goods | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: SAP Results Analysis - variance release based on good...
entry.content:
In my opinion you have two options:

- use period costing for production orders. Here you get WIP at target and variances for the period. Here you get WIP at target and variances for the period.

SAP: Product Cost by Order versus Product Cost by Period | SAP Help Portal

- activate WIP at actual prices in ML actual costing. This takes place both in Product Cost by Period and in Product Cost by Order.

You can even use both functionalities because they are not mutually exclusive. or just one of them. From the point of view of production that is already running, the second option may be easier. Both work, I have already used them on projects.
